docs.title: LinAlgZero
docs.text: This repository offers tools for generating a linear algebra problem dataset and training an open-source base model, aiming to explore its potential for emergent reasoning as inspired by the Deepseek-R1 paper.

docs.title: __init__(config)
docs.text:
Initialize the base trainer with configuration and setup core components.

Sets up device selection, session management, logging, and initializes all trainer components to None. Subclasses must implement abstract methods to create model, optimizer, dataloaders, metrics, and loss function.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default config ZeroConfig

Configuration object containing training parameters, model settings, and experiment configuration.

required

docs.title: compute_loss(model_output, batch) abstractmethod
docs.text:
Calculate the loss value for backpropagation.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model_output dict

Dictionary containing model predictions and outputs from forward_pass().

required batch dict

Original batch data containing targets and inputs.

required

Returns:

Type Description Tensor

torch.Tensor: Scalar loss tensor ready for backward() call.

docs.title: create_dataloaders() abstractmethod
docs.text:
Create training and validation dataloaders.

Returns:

Type Description tuple[DataLoader, DataLoader]

tuple[DataLoader, DataLoader]: Training and validation dataloaders (train_dataloader, val_dataloader) with appropriate batch sizes and data preprocessing.

docs.title: create_loss() abstractmethod
docs.text:
Create and return the loss function for training.

Returns:

Type Description Module

nn.Module: Loss function instance (e.g., CrossEntropyLoss, MSELoss) compatible with model outputs and targets.

docs.title: create_metrics() abstractmethod
docs.text:
Create training and validation metrics for performance tracking.

Returns:

Type Description tuple[dict[str, Metric], dict[str, Metric]]

tuple[dict[str, Metric], dict[str, Metric]]: Two dictionaries containing (train_metrics, val_metrics). Each metric should implement update(), compute(), and reset() methods.

docs.title: create_model() abstractmethod
docs.text:
Create and return the neural network model for training.

Returns:

Type Description Module

nn.Module: The model instance, ready for training. Should be compatible with the expected input/output format defined in forward_pass().

docs.title: create_optimizer() abstractmethod
docs.text:
Create and return the optimizer for model parameter updates.

Returns:

Name Type Description Optimizer Optimizer

Configured optimizer instance (e.g., Adam, SGD) with appropriate learning rate and parameters from self.model.parameters().

docs.title: forward_pass(batch) abstractmethod
docs.text:
Execute forward pass through the model with a batch of data.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default batch dict

Batch data dictionary containing inputs and targets, already moved to the appropriate device.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description dict dict

Model outputs dictionary containing predictions and any intermediate results needed for loss computation and metrics.

docs.title: train()
docs.text:
Execute the complete training loop with validation and checkpointing.

Runs setup(), then iterates through training steps for config.train_iterations. Handles periodic logging, validation, and checkpoint saving based on config intervals. Automatically manages model train/eval modes and W&B logging.

docs.title: visualize_batch(model_output, batch, mode) abstractmethod
docs.text:
Log visualizations of model predictions and data to W&B.

Called periodically during training and once per validation to provide visual feedback on model performance.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model_output dict

Model predictions and outputs from forward_pass().

required batch dict

Input batch data and targets.

required mode str

Either "train" or "val" to indicate the current phase.

required

docs.title: ZeroConfig dataclass
docs.text:
Dataclass that stores training configuration parameters.

Attributes:

Name Type Description batch_size int

Number of samples processed in each training batch.

train_iterations int

Total number of training iterations.

n_workers int

Number of workers for the dataloader.

gpu bool

Whether to use the GPU.

print_iterations int

Frequency (in iterations) at which to print training metrics to the console.

log_loss_iterations int

Frequency (in iterations) at which to log the training loss.

log_media_iterations int

Frequency (in iterations) at which to visualize samples and log media.

val_iterations int

Frequency (in iterations) at which to run the validation loop.

output_path str

The root directory for logs.

tag Optional[str]

A descriptive tag for the run.

restore_path Optional[str]

Path to a session directory to restore.

main_val_metric str

The primary validation metric for checkpointing.

learning_rate float

The learning rate for the optimizer.

weight_decay float

The weight decay for the optimizer.

wandb_project str

Project name for Weights & Biases.

wandb_entity Optional[str]

Entity for Weights & Biases.

wandb_run_name Optional[str]

Run name for Weights & Biases.
